chosen_primary_stack: TypeScript-first product stack on the current active Node LTS line, with Python retained for validators/tooling and Swift retained for the native macOS client.

hard_rules: semantic selectors remain first-class
route and shell continuity stays contract-authored
browser layout and design-token iteration remain rapid
runtime_roles.ownership_reason: Portal, collaboration, and governance surfaces are selector-heavy, route-rich, and Playwright-first.

hard_rules: money-bearing logic must use exact-decimal abstractions only
canonical hashing must be centralized
stream resume and command idempotency remain contract-shaped
runtime_roles.ownership_reason: Northbound command, snapshot, stream, and authority-adapter services benefit from sharing contract types and test fixtures with the browser layer.
runtime_roles.primary_runtime: TypeScript on current active Node LTS

hard_rules: closed schemas stay authoritative
canonical decimal strings stay cross-runtime stable
no handwritten DTO drift without an explicit adapter boundary
runtime_roles.ownership_reason: The product core should consume generated or tightly aligned TypeScript models while Python and Swift consume the same schema source through dedicated adapters.
runtime_roles.primary_runtime: JSON Schema as source, TypeScript as primary product-consumption target

hard_rules: Python remains the authoritative offline contract-validation toolchain for the corpus
tooling consumes schemas and golden fixtures rather than inventing parallel models
runtime_roles.ownership_reason: The repository already has a substantial Python validator, analysis, and forensic toolchain that should remain first-class rather than being rewritten prematurely.

hard_rules: tests reuse semantic selectors
fixtures stay contract-driven
browser automation remains mandatory regardless of backend runtime
runtime_roles.ownership_reason: Playwright is already present in the repo and is explicitly required by the UI/UX and browser-surface contracts.

hard_rules: server remains source of legal truth
native client stays projection-and-command only
SwiftUI default with AppKit for dense evidence surfaces
runtime_roles.ownership_reason: The macOS operator workspace is a first-class native embodiment and must not be reduced to a browser wrapper.

hard_rules: native semantic identifiers mirror browser semantic selectors where the contract says so
runtime_roles.ownership_reason: Native executable validation belongs in the design loop alongside Playwright for browser surfaces.

hard_rules: no gratuitous runtime proliferation
glue scripts should consume shared contracts rather than hard-coded ad hoc payloads
runtime_roles.ownership_reason: Avoid inventing a fourth first-class runtime for ordinary glue. Prefer the existing product-core runtime unless Python already owns the tool surface.
runtime_roles.primary_runtime: TypeScript for product-adjacent automation, Python where existing validator/analysis scripts already own the job

seam_rules.rule: Share schemas, canonical fixtures, and generated metadata; do not duplicate product business logic in Python except for offline verification and analysis packs.

seam_rules.boundary: TypeScript product core <-> Swift macOS client
seam_rules.rule: Share northbound contracts and generated models; keep legal truth, gate logic, and authority mutation semantics on the server.
Share contract-shaped DTOs and validation helpers, but keep UI state and server truth concerns separated.
